item.title: Mundys Plans to Boost Stake in Channel Tunnel Operator Getlink
item.description: Mundys SpA is close to unveiling a plan to increase its stake in Channel Tunnel operator Getlink SE, people with knowledge of the matter said, in a move aimed at deepening the Italian infrastructure group’s investments in France.

item.title: RBA Says Not Possible to Predict Cash Rate Path With Confidence
item.description: Australia’s central bank said it’s impossible to confidently predict the path for policy after raising interest rates for a second time this year as a surge in oil prices from the Middle East war fans inflation risks.

item.title: Asian Stocks Wipe Out 2026 Gains as War Drives Inflation Worries
item.description: Asian stocks fell, with the key regional benchmark erasing its gain for the year, as soaring energy costs due to the Iran war raise concerns of inflation and slower growth.

item.title: Activist Palliser Targets MSG Maker Ajinomoto in AI Hunt
item.description: UK-based activist fund Palliser Capital has expanded its hunt for overlooked AI beneficiaries in Japan with a stake in seasoning maker Ajinomoto Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chinese Stocks Fare Better Than Global Peers in Iran-Driven Rout
item.description: Chinese stocks are emerging as one of the best markets to ride out the Iran war, with their outperformance against global peers on track for the strongest since August 2025.

item.title: Asia Steps Up Bond Support as Energy Shock Sends Yields Surging
item.description: This month’s surge in Asian bond yields has triggered an increase in debt buying across the region, as governments seek to limit the spillover from higher energy prices to local borrowing costs.

item.title: China’s Top Private Insurer Taps AI to Unlock $174 Billion Value
item.description: Five years ago, almost no accident and health insurance claims at Ping An Insurance Group ran without humans. Today, nearly 60% are automated, with some settled in just 51 seconds.

item.title: Tokyo CPI Cools to Slowest in Two Years as Food Cost Gains Ease
item.description: Tokyo inflation cooled to its slowest pace in nearly two years as gains in food costs continued to slow.

item.title: Iran Strikes Fully Laden Kuwait Oil Tanker in Dubai Port
item.description: Iran hit a fully laden Kuwaiti oil tanker in the anchorage area of Dubai’s port, damaging the hull and starting a fire on board, state-run Kuwait Petroleum Corp. said in a statement.

item.title: India’s Soaring Oil Bill Shows Limits of RBI’s Rupee Defense
item.description: India’s heavy reliance on oil imports is straining the country’s current account deficit as the Iran war drags on, making it difficult for the central bank to steady the rupee despite the aggressive measures it’s taken so far to halt the slide.

item.title: Australia to Ban Credit, Debit Card Surcharges in Hit to Banks
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia won’t allow surcharges on debit and credit cards from October, a move that will hit banks’ revenue and bring the country in line with peers in Europe and the UK.

item.title: Korea’s $1 Trillion Pension to Flex Voting Power in Reform Push
item.description: South Korea’s $1 trillion pension fund will aggressively wield its voting rights to improve corporate governance and transparency, which have lagged global standards, its chief executive said.

item.title: UK Food Exports to US Slumped After Trump Imposed Trade Tariffs
item.description: UK exports to the US slumped in the second half of 2025, as tariffs raised the prices of products from whisky to infant formula.

item.title: Higher Prices Are Coming for British Shoppers, Retail Lobby Says
item.description: UK shoppers should brace for a wave of price increases, the retail industry warned as a survey taken immediately after the outbreak of the US-Iran war found higher energy costs had yet to filter through to household budgets.

item.title: Iran War Tensions Turn Once-Hot Korean Stock Trade Into Poor Bet
item.description: The bullish chorus on South Korean equities is cracking as the Iran war exposes the vulnerability of a rally narrowly built on a handful of growth stocks.

item.title: Group Plans $100 Million Midterm Effort to Back Trump AI Agenda
item.description: A former top aide to President Donald Trump is initiating a $100 million campaign to promote the administration’s pro-artificial intelligence agenda during this year’s midterm elections.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Fed’s Powell Says Long-Term Inflation in Check
item.description: Gold held two days of gains, after the Federal Reserve said long-term US inflation expectations appeared to be in check even as the war continues in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Extends Gains After Iran Attacks Crude Carrier: Markets Wrap
item.description: Crude oil extended gains and US equity-index futures dropped after Iran struck a Kuwaiti crude oil carrier in Dubai port as the war in the Middle East showed little sign of easing.

item.title: Netflix Is Testing Just How Much You Will Pay
item.description: The most popular Netflix plan will now cost $20 a month -- a 150% increase from 2013

item.title: Meta, TikTok Flagged for Potential Australia Kids’ Ban Breaches
item.description: Australia’s online safety regulator is investigating Meta Platforms Inc., Snap Inc., TikTok and YouTube for potentially failing to comply with the country’s social media ban for under-16s, in the first formal assessment of Big Tech’s response to the world-first crackdown.

item.title: Oil Extends Advance as Trump Threatens Escalation to Iran War
item.description: Oil pushed higher after US President Donald Trump threatened an escalation of hostilities against Iran, raising fears the war is far from abating and global markets are poised for further chaos.

item.title: Sun Life Doubles Down on Asset Management With Three Deals
item.description: Sun Life Financial Inc. paid more than C$2 billion ($1.4 billion) to complete its stakes in two investment managers as the Canadian life insurer makes a bigger push into the asset management space.

item.title: Mativ Holdings Prices $500 Million Junk Loan at Steep Discount
item.description: Banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. have sold a $500 million leveraged loan for Mativ Holdings Inc. at one of the biggest discounts so far this year, as investor demand for risky debt wanes amid volatile markets.

item.title: Fortress Plans to Restructure Discount Retailer Poundstretcher
item.description: Fortress Investment Group plans to restructure Poundstretcher Ltd. just two years after acquiring the struggling discount retailer, the latest sign of deepening strain across the UK high street.

item.title: Meta Testing a New Premium Subscription on Instagram with Stealth View on Stories
item.description: Instagram is testing a new premium subscription that gives paying users access to exclusive features, part of an effort to build a reliable revenue stream outside of the company’s core advertising business.

item.title: Fed’s Williams Says Policy Well Positioned Amid Supply Shock
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said interest rates were well positioned amid signs of significant supply chain disruptions due to war in the Middle East.

item.title: Suriname’s Ex-President Santokhi Dies, Left Office Last Year
item.description: Former Suriname President Chandrikapersad “Chan” Santokhi died unexpectedly on Monday, the government announced, after leading the small but oil-rich South American nation until last year.

item.title: Banco do Brasil Weighs Farmer Relief Amid War, Capital Risk
item.description: Banco do Brasil SA is weighing fresh financial relief for farmers hit by the war in Iran, a move that risks straining its balance sheet and reviving the need for asset sales or a capital increase.

item.title: Insider-Trading Scheme Born of Romance Snags Another Broker
item.description: A second former Spartan Capital Securities broker was accused of making hundreds of thousands of dollars on insider trades from tips linked to a man who stole confidential corporate deal information from his girlfriend’s laptop while they were working at home during the pandemic.

item.title: Obamacare Customers Paying $6,000 a Year Doubled in 2026
item.description: The share of Affordable Care Act insurance customers in plans that cost more than $6,000 a year doubled, a sign of the squeeze on household budgets after Congress let Covid-era assistance expire.
